skill.name: session-intelligence-harvester
skill.description: Extracts session learnings after productive AI work, classifies them, and guides updates to reusable project knowledge files.
skill.summary: Harvest session corrections and patterns into reusable project memory.
skill.author: 92Bilal26

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance findings are false positives caused by Markdown backticks and plain-text references. The real concern is semantic: the skill tells agents to edit target files and commit changes, which creates elevated filesystem and git-operation risk. No prompt injection attempt, credential access, network exfiltration, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: Broad File Modification and Commit Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to implement updates, edit each target file, verify edits, create a PHR, and commit changes. This is not malicious, but it can modify many project files if used without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly require file edits and commits. The risk is operational overreach rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misread as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited lines are Markdown code fences, templates, and inline file names. No executable shell expansion or Ruby code was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-number review shows Markdown examples and formatting blocks only. There is no script body, command substitution, or user-controlled execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Plain Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts are not backed by cryptographic code. The cited lines contain prose, headings, file names, routing examples, and report templates.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations do not implement hashes, ciphers, or password handling. The alerts appear to match incidental text such as Markdown and abbreviations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern in Questions
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts point to ordinary analysis prompts that ask why an error occurred and what was done wrong. No host inspection command or system enumeration request was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is a reflective workflow question, not a command. There is no evidence of operating system or network reconnaissance.

dangerous_patterns.title: Agent-Directed Repository Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs the agent to read target files, apply edits, verify changes, and finish with a commit. Users should review target files and diffs before allowing this workflow to complete.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is directly stated in the workflow. It is risky in scope but lacks indicators of stealth, exfiltration, or destructive intent.

content.user_title: Harvest Session Learnings Into Team Memory
content.value_statement: AI sessions often solve problems once, then lose the lesson. This skill converts corrections, patterns, and validation gaps into reusable project knowledge.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes completed sessions for corrections, patterns, and missing checks.
Classifies learnings by target knowledge component and trigger point.
Guides agents to read target files before proposing or applying edits.
Creates structured harvest summaries with learnings, targets, and statuses.
Supports documentation of permanent project knowledge after repeated fixes.
limitations: Requires existing project knowledge files and clear component boundaries.
Can produce broad edits if the session includes many related learnings.
Does not validate domain truth beyond files and session context provided.
Commit steps should be reviewed before publication or shared repository use.

use_cases.title: Capture recurring correction patterns
use_cases.description: Turn repeated AI corrections into durable project guidance and validation checks.

use_cases.title: Maintain agent operating knowledge
use_cases.description: Route new workflow rules into the files where future agents will find them.

use_cases.title: Document lessons after delivery work
use_cases.description: Summarize what changed, why it mattered, and where the lesson was encoded.

prompt_templates.title: Basic session harvest
prompt_templates.scenario: Use after a focused work session with one clear correction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Harvest learnings from this session. Identify the correction, classify it, and suggest the target project knowledge file.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-file pattern capture
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when the same issue appeared in several files or workflows.
prompt_templates.prompt: Capture session intelligence from these related fixes. Group the patterns, choose target components, and show the proposed updates.

prompt_templates.title: Validation gap analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when a missed check caused rework or format drift.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this session for validation gaps. Explain what check would have caught the issue and where it should be encoded.

prompt_templates.title: Full implementation harvest
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when the user approves direct updates to project knowledge files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Harvest this session end to end. Read target files, apply approved updates, verify changes, and summarize every encoded learning.

output_examples.input: We fixed a repeated format drift in three agent files.
output: Learning identified: repeated format drift across agent files.
Classification: agent convergence pattern and validation gap.
Recommended target: agent guidance plus workflow validation checklist.
Expected result: future agents check the canonical format before final output.

output_examples.input: A session revealed that prerequisite files were not read before planning.
output: Expected result: planning starts with required prerequisite review.

best_practices: Use the skill after concrete corrections, not after routine conversations.
Review target files and proposed edits before allowing broad repository changes.
Keep each harvested learning tied to a trigger, target, and prevention outcome.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to rewrite project standards without a real session lesson.
Do not route every learning into one central file when specialized files exist.
Do not skip diff review before committing harvested knowledge changes.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It turns session corrections and patterns into durable project knowledge updates.

faq.question: Does it edit files automatically?
faq.answer: The workflow instructs agents to edit files when approved. Users should review target files and diffs.

faq.question: Is this a security skill?
faq.answer: No. It is a productivity and knowledge-management skill with elevated filesystem risk.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it after a session with corrected drift, repeated mistakes, missing checks, or reusable workflow lessons.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: What should I review before committing?
faq.answer: Review the selected target files, exact insertions, generated summary, and repository diff.
